_comment: S11 cost worksheet (storage contract §7). The number the company watches -- cost per billion
events retained -- as a MEASURED output, not a slide. Bytes per row are measured by the
two-tier bench accounting (crates/prism-engine/src/bench.rs; committed baselines.json), then
scaled to per-million and per-billion events. A byte is a byte; the dollar cost is a function
of the backend's storage price, which is why this worksheet reports BYTES and leaves the price
multiplication to the operator with their contract.
Charter honesty: this is BACKEND-CONDITIONAL. These bytes are the on-disk/on-object footprint,
which is the same on a local object store and on S3; the REQUEST and EGRESS costs, and any
compression the backend applies, are not, and are tagged as measured against the local backend
until MinIO/S3 is exercised (the filed increment).
_dimensionality_warning: The measured numbers below are at dim=64 -- the TEST config, NOT a product number. Real
embedding models are ~768-dimensional, where the cold tier (exact float32 vectors) is
768*4 = 3072 bytes = 3.07 KB per vector, ~12x the dim=64 figure. The README and any external
material MUST quote the 768d numbers (projected_at_768d below), never the dim=64 ones.
A measured-at-768d worksheet is a filed obligation (measured_at_768d), unlocked by the first
real-embedding corpus -- the SAME C-3 standing item (issue #3) that unlocks the real-embedding
index re-derivation. One corpus, both obligations.

projected_at_768d._rule: PROJECTION, not a measurement: the cold tier scales linearly with dimension (exact float32 = dim*4 bytes/vector); the hot PQ tier does not (pq_m bytes, fixed). At dim=768, pq_m=8: PQ = 8 B/vector unchanged; exact vectors = 768*4 = 3072 B = 3.07 KB/vector. Scalars_and_text are dimension-independent (~284 B/row). These are the numbers the README quotes.
projected_at_768d.note: 1e9 events x 3072 B cold = 3072 GB (~3 TB) of exact vectors per billion events; the hot scan tier stays ~292 GB. rerank_tier_multiple at 768d = 3072/8 = 384x.

measured_at_768d.filed: unlocked by the first real-embedding corpus (issue #3, C-3 standing item)
measured_at_768d._rule: The projection above assumes float32 exact vectors and no backend compression. A measured-at-768d worksheet against a real-embedding corpus on the MinIO/S3 backend replaces the projection, and any material quoting a 768d number switches to the measured one the moment it exists.

bytes_per_row._note: pq_m bytes for the PQ codes; dim*4 for the exact float32 vectors; the rest is the scalar columns and the bodies. rerank_tier_multiple = exact / pq = 32x.

tiers._rule: Hot tier = the scan path (PQ codes + scalar columns), always resident/cached and read on every query. Cold tier = the rerank path (exact vectors) plus the bodies, fetched only for survivors/returns. Bodies currently live inside scalars_and_text in the bench accounting; separating them out is a filed refinement, so the split below assigns exact_vectors to cold and the rest to hot, which is the conservative reading for cold-tier egress.

per_billion_events._note: 1e9 events x 548.36 B/event = 548.36 GB total retained. The cold tier -- the expensive-to-egress exact vectors -- is 256 GB of that. The scan tier a query always touches is the 292 GB hot half.

request_counts._rule: Cold-tier object requests per query, from Counters.object_requests: one per part whose exact vectors are fetched, coalesced ranged reads within a part counting as one. A query touching P eligible parts issues <= P cold requests; the fetch budget (§6) bounds the bytes those requests move.

measured._source: crates/prism-cli/tests/storage.rs::cold_path_request_counts_are_measured_and_bounded (permanent, storage CI job) — prints the numbers below; the S11 MinIO gate exercises the same path against a real remote.
measured._backend_independent: object_requests and retrieved_bytes are a function of the query and the data, NOT the backend: cold_read_vectors fetches the same rows from the same parts whether the bytes come from local disk or MinIO (answer-invariance §3). So this measurement on the local backend is the number the MinIO gate reports.
measured._note: 40 rerank survivors x 64 dim x 4 B = 10240 B, coalesced into 1 object request (all survivors in one part); 1 <= 4 eligible parts, the per-part bound holds.

projected_at_768d._rule: PROJECTION: object_requests is dimension-independent (one per part touched); retrieved_bytes scales linearly with dimension (40 x 768 x 4).
